emails.subject: Visit Summary - Biscuit - November 15, 2025
emails.content:
Hi Kai,

Here's the summary from Biscuit's visit today.

Patient: Biscuit (Canine, Mixed Breed, ~5 years, 42 lbs)

Exam Findings:
- Overall in great shape. Weight is good, heart and lungs sound clear.
- Teeth have moderate tartar buildup, especially on the upper molars. I'd recommend a dental cleaning within the next 2-3 months to prevent any gum issues.
- Ears, eyes, skin all normal. No concerns.

Vaccinations Administered:
- Rabies (3-year)
- DHPP Booster
- Bordetella
- Lyme

Next Steps:
- Schedule a dental cleaning in January or February. We'll need to do pre-anesthesia bloodwork, which we can do same day.
- Continue heartworm prevention monthly.
- Annual exam due again November 2026.

Biscuit was a very good boy. Give him an extra treat tonight.

Dr. Sarah Chen, DVM
JP Animal Hospital
(617) 555-0485

- misc budget has like $80-100 of flex most months
- spread across dec/jan/feb = ~$175/month extra needed

emails.subject: Your Nutrition Plan - Follow Up from January 20 Visit
emails.content:
Hi Kai,

Good meeting you today. Here's a summary of what we covered and the plan going forward.

Current status:
- HbA1c 6.0% (up from 5.8%)
- Diet is generally strong as a pescatarian but carbohydrate portions are higher than ideal
- Sedentary work hours are a factor

Dietary changes to focus on:
1. Switch white rice for brown rice, quinoa, or cauliflower rice
2. Use the plate method at meals: 1/2 vegetables, 1/4 protein, 1/4 starch
3. Increase fiber to 25-30g daily (lentils, beans, leafy greens)
4. Add fatty fish 2-3 times per week (salmon, sardines, mackerel)
5. Replace fruit juice with whole fruit
6. Front-load carbohydrates earlier in the day when possible

I'd recommend tracking your meals for at least the first 2-3 weeks to identify patterns. MyFitnessPal works well for this.

We'll schedule a follow-up in about a month to see how things are going. Your next bloodwork with Dr. Nguyen will give us the real data point.

Feel free to reach out if questions come up between now and then.

Rachel Lim, RD
Jamaica Plain Community Health Center
(617) 555-0762
